ucs.title: [LLM] INC Ransom encryptor execution via PsExec/WMIC with characteristic CLI flags (--file/--dir/--motd/--skip-hidden)
ucs.description: Hunts the INC Ransom (GOLD IONIC) payload's distinctive command-line invocation pattern. The INC encryptor is routinely pushed across the estate by the operators using PsExec or wmic process call create and is run with bespoke switches such as --file, --dir, --motd, --debug, --sup, --lhd, --skip-hidden, --skip-files, --ens — a fingerprint not shared by mainstream commodity ransomware families.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("psexesvc.exe","PSEXESVC.exe","wmiprvse.exe","services.exe","cmd.exe") OR Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe") (Processes.process="*--file*" OR Processes.process="*--dir*" OR Processes.process="*--motd*" OR Processes.process="*--skip-hidden*" OR Processes.process="*--skip-files*" OR Processes.process="*--ens*" OR Processes.process="*--lhd*" OR Processes.process="*--sup*" OR Processes.process="*--debug*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process, "(?i)(--file\s+|--dir\s+|--motd\s+|--skip-hidden|--skip-files|--ens\s+|--lhd|--sup\s+\d|--debug)") | rex field=process "(?<inc_flags>--(file|dir|motd|skip-hidden|skip-files|ens|lhd|sup|debug))" | stats values(inc_flags) as flags_seen values(process) as cmdlines values(parent_process_name) as parents min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime count by dest user process_name | where mvcount(flags_seen) >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any ("psexesvc.exe","PSEXEC.exe","wmiprvse.exe","services.exe","cmd.exe","explorer.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("--file ","--dir ","--motd ","--skip-hidden","--skip-files","--ens ","--lhd","--sup ","--debug")
| extend FlagHits = countof(ProcessCommandLine, "--")
| extend IncFlags = extract_all(@"--(file|dir|motd|skip-hidden|skip-files|ens|lhd|sup|debug)\b", ProcessCommandLine)
| where array_length(IncFlags) >= 2
| where not(FileName has_any ("msbuild.exe","dotnet.exe","npm.exe","git.exe","python.exe","node.exe"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, IncFlags, SHA256
| sort by Timestamp asc
ucs.rationale: These specific switches (--motd, --skip-hidden, --lhd, --ens, --sup) are documented in SentinelOne, Huntress and SecureWorks reporting on GOLD IONIC's INC encryptor and are not a generic ransomware indicator. Combined with the characteristic PsExec/WMIC parent (their published lateral-movement TTP) the rule fires on INC's actual encryption stage rather than a vague 'ransomware-like' pattern.

ucs.title: [LLM] INC Ransom pre-exfil staging: 7-Zip archive of file shares followed by MEGAsync/rclone egress
ucs.description: Hunts INC Ransom's (GOLD IONIC) double-extortion exfiltration chain. The crew is consistently observed running 7z.exe / 7za.exe to archive enumerated SMB share contents to a staging directory (T1074, T1560.001) and then pushing those archives to MEGA via MEGAsync.exe or to other cloud storage via rclone.exe (T1567 / T1537), often within hours of the encryptor running.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("7z.exe","7za.exe","WinRAR.exe","Rar.exe") Processes.process IN ("*a -*","*-mx*","*-p*","*\\\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host | join type=left host [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as exfil_first max(_time) as exfil_last from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("MEGAsync.exe","megacmd.exe","rclone.exe","WinSCP.exe","FileZilla.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host process_name as exfil_tool process as exfil_cmd] | where isnotnull(exfil_tool) AND (exfil_first - lastTime) < 86400 AND (exfil_first - lastTime) > -3600 | table firstTime host user process_name process exfil_tool exfil_cmd exfil_first | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(exfil_first)`
ucs.kql:
let archive =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ ("7z.exe","7za.exe","WinRAR.exe","Rar.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" a "," a -","-mx","-p") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\\\",".7z",".rar",".zip")
    | project ArchiveTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ArchiveAccount=AccountName, ArchiveCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ArchiveBin=FileName;
let exfil =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ ("MEGAsync.exe","megacmd.exe","MEGAcmdServer.exe","rclone.exe","WinSCP.exe","FileZilla.exe","putty.exe","pscp.exe")
    | project ExfilTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ExfilBin=FileName, ExfilCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
archive
| join kind=inner exfil on DeviceId
| where ExfilTime between (ArchiveTime .. ArchiveTime + 24h)
| extend MinutesBetween = datetime_diff('minute', ExfilTime, ArchiveTime)
| project ArchiveTime, ExfilTime, MinutesBetween, DeviceName, ArchiveAccount, ArchiveBin, ArchiveCmd, ExfilBin, ExfilCmd
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("mega.nz","mega.io","userstorage.mega.co.nz","g.api.mega.co.nz")
    | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
) on DeviceName
| sort by ArchiveTime asc
ucs.rationale: MEGA via MEGAsync is INC Ransom's signature exfil channel called out by SecureWorks (GOLD IONIC), Huntress and ReliaQuest, and the 7-Zip-then-MEGA sequence is a specific INC tradecraft pattern (not just any actor that touches MEGA). Time-correlating archive creation with cloud egress on the same host within 24h gives a high-signal chain hit on INC's exfil stage before the encryptor lands, while ke
